pi_items.source: Task description (task.json line 5-6) and verified in verifier.py line 53 which checks for 'activate lockdown' in trigger_name.lower()
Task description (task.json line 6) and verified in verifier.py line 61 which checks for 'security alert: lockdown' in action_text.lower()
Task description states 'Duration: At least 5 seconds'. 5 seconds = 5000 milliseconds. Verified in verifier.py line 69 which checks if duration >= 5000

Endpoint is used consistently throughout task scripts: setup_task.sh line 17, 30, 36 and export_result.sh line 19. However, public Nx Witness documentation references /rest/v1/rules and /rest/v4/events/rules endpoints, not specifically /rest/v1/eventRules

privileged_info_summary: The task configures an emergency alert system in Nx Witness VMS with a Soft Trigger event rule named 'Activate Lockdown' that displays the text overlay 'SECURITY ALERT: LOCKDOWN' with a minimum duration of 5 seconds (5000 milliseconds). The task uses the /rest/v1/eventRules API endpoint to manage event rules, with a verification system that checks for the correct trigger name (case-insensitive), action text (case-insensitive), and minimum duration through a Python-based verifier that parses API responses.
